lifecycles._note: Direct Connect + routing-rule CRUD (Wave 2, NOT runtime-proven, HEAVY/billable). A direct-connect attaches to a base VPC: the session-shared VPC (adopt:vpc, per knowledge/vpc-scheduling-strategy.md — does NOT self-create a VPC). Then: create-dc -> get/set-dc, create-routing-rule -> delete-routing-rule, delete-dc. All dc/routing-rule writes are one optional 'dc' group so a bad body skips only this family (cleaned up children-first). Bodies from data/api_docs.json request_example. create-dc returns $.direct_connect.id; create-routing-rule returns $.routing_rule.id (both soft-captured; dependent steps reference {direct_connect_id}/{routing_rule_id} -> real chain on success, literal-but-norm-matching on 4xx so coverage still records). NOTE: the docs createdirectconnect example sends tags as a STRINGIFIED list ("[{'key':...}]") which is almost certainly a doc bug; we send a proper JSON list [] (the engine merges owner tags). bandwidth=1 (Gbps?) is a docs guess; routing-rule destination_type=ON_PREMISE with destination_cidr; destination_resource_id omitted (we have no on-premise resource) which may 4xx -> optional/tolerant. USERGUIDE FACTS APPLIED 2026-06-11 (knowledge/formal/services/networking__direct-connect.yaml, pending live validation): DC name must be 3-20 chars, upper/lowercase LETTERS+DIGITS ONLY (no hyphens/underscores) — regrdc{ualpha} (14 alnum chars) complies; uplink bandwidth enum is 1G|10G so bandwidth=1 stays a guess about the field encoding; max 5 DCs/account; ONE DC per VPC (1:1) — encoded as the negative step create-direct-connect-duplicate-must-4xx (expects [400,409,422] ONLY, the engine treats expect_status as exhaustive so an unexpected 2xx fails the group = platform regression signal); Security Group is a documented prerequisite SERVICE of DC but is not a create-body field, so none is created here. Covers all 5 direct-connect WRITE ops: POST /v1/direct-connects, PUT /v1/direct-connects/{direct_connect_id}, DELETE /v1/direct-connects/{direct_connect_id}, POST /v1/direct-connects/{direct_connect_id}/routing-rules, DELETE /v1/direct-connects/{direct_connect_id}/routing-rules/{routing_rule_id}.

steps.name: create-direct-connect-duplicate-must-4xx
steps._note: NEGATIVE expectation (userguide dc-one-per-vpc, provenance docs): only ONE Direct Connect per VPC in a service zone, so a SECOND create on the same (shared) VPC MUST 4xx — this step expects ONLY [400,409,422] and fails the 'dc' group if the platform ever lets it through. Runs inside group 'dc' so it only fires when the first create actually succeeded (group-skip otherwise, i.e. no false positive against an empty VPC). Deliberately carries no cleanup: on the documented behaviour nothing is created; in the regression case (unexpected 2xx) the duplicate would leak until the name-prefix sweep (name regrdcdup{ualpha} = 17 alnum chars, within the 3-20 letters+digits-only rule). Pending live validation.

json.description: API regression suite (duplicate on same VPC must fail)
json.name: regrdcdup{ualpha}
